metadata.release_id: TBD — research pack, not yet bound to a numbered Vexa release

release_id: TBD — research pack, not yet bound to a numbered Vexa release

sections.Blast radius:
- MS Teams bot audio pipeline: one new seam (diarization step) between the existing single-channel buffer and the transcription-service client. Output of the seam is `speaker_0..N` labels attached as metadata; the bytes sent to the transcription service are unchanged in stage 1.
- New RnD harness around the bot's audio slice (hot-reload runner + adapters + dashboard + autonomous evaluator). Lives in the repo but does NOT ship in any container.
- New evaluation tooling (MVP1+): pseudo-oracle metric runner (MVP1), oracle-seeded synthetic corpus generator (MVP2: offline pyannote + Whisper-large + voice-cloned TTS + augmentation), anchor + mutator + multi-diarizer (MVP3).
- No change to: transcription service, gmeet/zoom paths, dashboard product UI (RnD dashboard is a separate surface), Compose/Lite/Helm deploy artifacts, public APIs, DB schemas.
- Support / on-call surface: none (RnD only, no production users touch this in stage 1).
sections.CEO outcome: Vexa has a credible internal answer to MS Teams' weakest user-visible quality complaint (mis-attributed or unattributed speakers in transcripts), delivered in MVP-laddered increments so a working demo exists from MVP0 onward and every later MVP additively raises rigor — reproducible numbers (MVP1), frame-perfect ground truth (MVP2), and defensible diarizer-backend characterization (MVP3) — without ever leaving 'walking skeleton' state.
sections.CTO outcome: Each MVP is a full minimal product on its own (demo → autonomous metric → real ground truth → characterized backend choice). The production bot's audio pipeline carries a local diarization seam from MVP0; the RnD harness around it runs production code hot-reload outside the container; stage 2 (identity binding / TS-VAD / per-speaker routing) inherits a quantified floor, an extractability audit, a documented retroactive-correction UX decision, and a defensible backend pick — without architectural drift.
sections.Compose validation gate: Not required at any MVP. This pack does not modify any Compose-shipped service contract; the RnD harness runs outside Compose. If MVP3's final diff incidentally touches a file the Compose stack also imports, run the standard Compose smoke as a regression check; otherwise mark explicitly not required in PR evidence.
sections.Data / schema / API / public-contract de:
- Public APIs: no change. Transcription service interface, bot ↔ service RPC, dashboard product API all unchanged in stage 1.
- Internal contract (NEW, lands at MVP0): a stable seam inside the bot exposing `audio_frames_in (pcm, sample_rate, wall_clock_ts) → (segments, speaker_labels speaker_0..N, transcripts)`. Same codepath behind adapters added across MVPs (MVP0: tab capture; MVP1: file replay; live-bot stub at the seam from MVP0 but not wired). Diarizer backend swappable at one composition root from MVP0; alternatives wired in MVP3.
- Metadata: transcription requests/results carry an optional `speaker_label` field populated by the diarization step. Stage 1 keeps this internal to the bot/RnD harness — not exposed downstream.
- No DB schema changes. No new public endpoints. No breaking changes to any service contract.
- Decision still open inside the pack (must be answered by MVP3 close): retroactive-correction UX — does the dashboard render live (sometimes-wrong) labels and update them as the diarizer refines, or buffer and only emit committed labels? MVP0 ships whichever default is fastest to build; MVP3 records the deliberate choice.
sections.Explicitly out of scope:
- Identity binding to real participant names (Teams bluebox / roster / Copilot signals). Stage 2.
- Target-Speaker VAD (TS-VAD) and per-speaker channel routing in the bot. Stage 2.
- Changes to the transcription service. The service is called unchanged via the bot's existing client; Whisper weights stay inside the service.
- Production Teams bot integration / rollout / feature flagging.
- Container, Compose, Helm, Lite packaging of the RnD harness. The harness is hot-reload of production bot code outside any container; packaging is stage 2+.
- Setting a latency target. Latency is reported as an output (p50/p99 per backend), not gated as a pass/fail in this pack.
- gmeet / zoom diarization changes. Single-channel-only problem here.
- Live bot capture wiring of the third adapter. The adapter seam is defined; only tab-capture (MVP0) and file-replay (MVP1+) adapters are exercised in this pack.
sections.Included raw issues / PRs:
- No raw GitHub issues; this pack is a forward-looking research epic synthesized from a design conversation on MS Teams single-channel transcription + speaker attribution limits.
- Adjacent context (informational, not in scope): existing MS Teams bot audio buffering + transcription-service call path; existing gmeet/zoom per-participant channel routing path (kept as the target architecture once stage 2 introduces per-speaker routing).
sections.Isolation requirements:
- One branch / worktree for this pack only.
- Evidence root: `.agents/packs/pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd/`.
- Per-MVP evidence subfolders under the evidence root (`mvp0/`, `mvp1/`, `mvp2/`, `mvp3/`) so each MVP's deliverables are independently auditable and the pack PR can show the additive layering.
- Runtime namespace: `pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd` (used only for any local processes / ports the harness spins up; no Compose/Lite/Helm namespacing required).
- Hot-reload harness runs production bot code paths byte-identically — no parallel prototype implementation of the audio pipeline, at any MVP.
- Diarization model weights cached across reloads (process-supervisor pattern or equivalent) so iteration is not bottlenecked by model load. Required from MVP0.
- Transcription service is a stable backend dependency: harness points the bot's existing transcription-service client at a local or shared dev instance. Service is not modified, redeployed, or forked by this pack.
- Do not merge another pack into this lane to make any MVP pass.
- Do not introduce identity-binding / TS-VAD code to make a metric move; those are stage 2 and entering them here invalidates the floor characterization this pack is supposed to deliver.
sections.Lite validation gate: Not required at any MVP. No Lite-relevant surface changes. Mark explicitly not required in PR evidence.
sections.Live / human validation gate:
Required, laddered across MVPs:
- **MVP0:** 1 YouTube video eyeball pass via tab-capture → dashboard. Qualitative: is the demo plausible enough to keep building on?
- **MVP1, MVP2:** no additional human gate (autonomous-loop MVPs).
- **MVP3:** 3 distinct YouTube videos (panel, podcast, debate-style) via tab-capture; user judges, per video, whether speaker boundaries and transcript text are coherent enough to demonstrate the approach. Pass/fail recorded with short notes.
- No production Teams meeting test in this pack at any MVP. Live-Teams validation belongs to stage 3 (production integration).
sections.MVP ladder:
Delivery is a strict additive ladder. Each MVP is a fully demoable minimal product on its own — the pack can stop at any MVP if signal is sufficient — and every later MVP runs on top of the previous one's working artifacts. No parallel branches, no "will be integrated later" debt.

### MVP0 — Walking skeleton (end-to-end demo, no metrics)

- **Includes:** hot-reload harness over production bot code; diarization seam inside the bot; one diarizer wired (pyannote 3.x streaming) with the swap composition root in place; tab-capture adapter (browser → harness); live-bot adapter as a stub at the same seam (not wired); RnD dashboard rendering live `speaker_0..N` labels + transcripts via the bot's existing transcription-service client.
- **Excludes:** file-replay adapter, metrics, synthetic corpus, anchor set, alternative diarizers, extractability audit deliverable, retroactive-correction UX decision.
- **Demo:** user shares 1 YouTube panel video; dashboard shows live diarized transcript with plausible speaker boundaries.
- **Stoppable if:** the demo is an obvious go/no-go for the whole research direction. No metrics needed to call it.
- **Evidence:** `mvp0/` — harness command, dashboard screenshot or short capture, bot-seam diff summary.

### MVP1 — Autonomous iteration (add metrics on real audio)

- **Includes:** file-replay adapter (byte-identical codepath to tab-capture, same composition root); pseudo-oracle metric runner — DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 measured against the offline pyannote + Whisper-large output on a small real-audio set (3+ inputs: panel, podcast, debate).
- **Excludes:** synthetic regeneration, anchor set, alternative diarizers, mutators.
- **Demo:** every change produces a number. Codex can iterate without a human watching.
- **Stoppable if:** pseudo-oracle numbers alone are convincing enough to make the stage 2 call. Synthetic frame-perfect ground truth is nice-to-have, not required, if the qualitative MVP0 demo plus MVP1 numbers already justify stage 2.
- **Evidence:** `mvp1/` — metric runner command, results table, input audio manifest, pseudo-oracle output artifacts.

### MVP2 — Real ground truth (oracle-as-script-generator)

- **Includes:** script extraction from MVP1's pseudo-oracle output → voice-cloned TTS per speaker (XTTS-v2 / F5-TTS / equivalent) → original-timing stitch → augmentation pass (Opus codec round-trip at Teams bitrate, simulated packet loss, room IR convolution, mic noise floor). Synthetic audio's ground truth IS the script by construction. Metric runner now evaluates against true labels on synthetic.
- **Excludes:** anchor set, mutators, alternative diarizers.
- **Demo:** synthetic corpus exists, reproducibly regeneratable, evaluator emits DER/cpWER/latency against frame-perfect labels.
- **Stoppable if:** synthetic numbers are strong enough to architect stage 2 confidently. Multi-diarizer comparison can be deferred to stage 2's planning if MVP2's chosen default is already in the right ballpark.
- **Evidence:** `mvp2/` — generator command, synthetic corpus WAV + ground-truth scripts (or reproducible regeneration command), evaluator results on synthetic.

### MVP3 — Honest characterization + pack close

- **Includes:** anchor set (AMI headset mix or VoxConverse subset, ~30 min total) wired into the evaluator; overlap-injection mutator exercised on at least one MVP2 corpus item; at least one alternative diarizer (Diart or NeMo online clustering / Sortformer) wired into the swap composition root and characterized; bot-audio-pipeline extractability audit recorded; retroactive-correction UX decision picked and documented; 3 YouTube eyeball passes recorded with qualitative notes; Hardenloop run for the pack; PR opened against the pack's integration branch.
- **Excludes:** stage 2 work (identity binding, TS-VAD, per-speaker routing, container packaging).
- **Demo:** per-diarizer DER/cpWER/latency table on synthetic + anchor + mutated-overlap subset; synthetic-vs-anchor delta reported; extractability audit and UX decision are written-down artifacts in the PR.
- **Stoppable if:** this is the pack-close MVP. Stopping here = pack delivered.
- **Evidence:** `mvp3/` — backend comparison table, anchor results, mutator results, extractability audit note, UX decision note, Hardenloop output, eyeball-pass notes.
sections.PR readiness checklist:
Checklist is MVP-gated. The pack PR aggregates evidence across all MVPs.

**Cross-cutting (must hold at MVP3 / PR time):**
- [ ] Pack branch starts from the agreed base and stays scoped to this pack only.
- [ ] Bot audio pipeline modified at exactly one seam; no parallel prototype path; production bot code is what the harness runs at every MVP.
- [ ] Out-of-scope items confirmed absent from the diff (no bluebox, no TS-VAD, no per-speaker routing, no transcription-service changes).
- [ ] Per-MVP evidence subfolders (`mvp0/`, `mvp1/`, `mvp2/`, `mvp3/`) exist under `.agents/packs/pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd/` and contain the deliverables listed below.
- [ ] PR body links this epic and the evidence root.

**MVP0 — walking skeleton:**
- [ ] Hot-reload harness over production bot code is runnable; model weights cache across reloads.
- [ ] Diarization seam inside the bot exists at one composition root; swap point for backends is in place.
- [ ] pyannote 3.x streaming wired as the default diarizer.
- [ ] Tab-capture adapter wired; live-bot adapter present as a stub at the same seam (not wired).
- [ ] RnD dashboard renders live `speaker_0..N` + transcript from tab-capture.
- [ ] 1 YouTube eyeball pass recorded.

**MVP1 — autonomous metrics on real audio:**
- [ ] File-replay adapter wired; codepath byte-identical to tab-capture.
- [ ] Pseudo-oracle (offline pyannote + Whisper-large) producible reproducibly on the input set.
- [ ] Metric runner emits DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against pseudo-oracle on 3+ real-audio inputs.
- [ ] Results table recorded under `mvp1/`.

**MVP2 — frame-perfect ground truth:**
- [ ] Script extraction from pseudo-oracle output runs end-to-end.
- [ ] Voice-cloned TTS per speaker + original-timing stitcher produces synthetic WAV.
- [ ] Augmentation pass (Opus codec round-trip, simulated packet loss, room IR, mic noise) applied and configurable.
- [ ] Synthetic audio + ground-truth script artifacts stored under `mvp2/` (or reproducible regeneration command).
- [ ] Metric runner emits DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against synthetic frame-perfect labels.

**MVP3 — honest characterization + pack close:**
- [ ] Anchor set (AMI headset mix or VoxConverse subset, ~30 min) wired into the evaluator.
- [ ] Anchor numbers are within published-baseline ballpark for the chosen diarizer (sanity check on the harness).
- [ ] Overlap-injection mutator exercised on at least one MVP2 corpus item; results recorded.
- [ ] At least one alternative diarizer (Diart or NeMo online clustering / Sortformer) wired into the swap composition root and characterized.
- [ ] Per-diarizer-backend DER/cpWER/latency table recorded.
- [ ] Synthetic-vs-anchor delta reported with a written interpretation.
- [ ] Bot-audio-pipeline extractability audit recorded (clean seam confirmed, or required decoupling work itemized).
- [ ] Retroactive-correction UX decision documented (live-with-updates vs buffered-commit).
- [ ] 3 YouTube eyeball passes recorded with qualitative notes.
- [ ] Hardenloop run for the pack (RnD-only blast radius noted; no production exposure asserted).
sections.Pack metadata:
- Pack id: `pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd`
- Release: `TBD — research pack, not yet bound to a numbered Vexa release`
- Base branch: `main`
- Integration branch: `main`
- Runtime namespace: `pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd`
- Evidence root: `.agents/packs/pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd/`
sections.Stitching risk notes:
- Low stitch risk: the pack's only product-code change is one new seam inside the bot, with metadata that downstream consumers ignore in stage 1. The new seam is additive; existing single-channel transcription path continues unchanged across every MVP.
- Walking-skeleton property is load-bearing: every MVP must remain demoable after the next is layered in. CI / smoke for MVP0 must not regress when MVP1's file adapter lands; MVP0 + MVP1 must not regress when MVP2's synthetic generator lands; etc. PR-time check should re-run each MVP's demo or smoke.
- The RnD harness, oracle, synthetic generator, evaluator, and dashboard are net-new files under their own subtree and do not collide with other packs by surface.
- Risk to watch: if the bot-audio-extractability audit (MVP3 deliverable) finds entanglement and stage 1 has to refactor the audio path to expose a clean seam, the diff stops being additive and starts touching shared bot lifecycle code. In that case, the refactor must be small, reviewed independently, called out in the PR, and probably committed against MVP0's deliverables (since the seam is MVP0's contract) — do not co-mingle it with later-MVP wiring under a single commit.
- Risk to watch: diarizer dependency footprint (pyannote, torch, possibly NeMo) can balloon the bot image in later stages. Stage 1 runs hot-reload outside the container so this does not bite here, but pack notes (MVP3 deliverable) must record the dependency set chosen so stage 2 can plan packaging.
- This epic is not a release sign-off; it is the delivery contract for one independently reviewable research pack whose output feeds stage 2 (identity binding) design.
sections.Synthetic validation gate:
PRIMARY gate for the pack, laddered across MVPs:
- **MVP0:** not exercised — MVP0 is human-eyeball only.
- **MVP1:** pseudo-oracle metrics — DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against offline pyannote + Whisper-large on a small real-audio set (3+ inputs: panel, podcast, debate). Recorded under `mvp1/`.
- **MVP2:** frame-perfect synthetic metrics — DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against the oracle-seeded TTS-regenerated corpus with augmentation. Recorded under `mvp2/`.
- **MVP3:** anchor + mutator + multi-diarizer — same metrics against AMI/VoxConverse subset (sanity), against the overlap-injection-mutated subset (overlap stress), and per-diarizer-backend (at minimum: pyannote 3.x streaming + one alternative). Synthetic-vs-anchor delta reported. Recorded under `mvp3/`.
- **Pack-close pass criterion:** all three (MVP1/MVP2/MVP3) metric tiers produced and recorded; no fixed numeric threshold in stage 1, but the delta-to-anchor must be reported with a stated interpretation.
sections.User outcome: From MVP0 onward, a user can share any YouTube tab in the RnD dashboard and see a real-time diarized transcript with stable `speaker_0..speaker_N` labels. Each later MVP keeps that demo working while adding rigor underneath it.

- [ ] Results table recorded under `mvp1/`.

**MVP2 — frame-perfect ground truth:**
- [ ] Script extraction from pseudo-oracle output runs end-to-end.
- [ ] Voice-cloned TTS per speaker + original-timing stitcher produces synthetic WAV.
- [ ] Augmentation pass (Opus codec round-trip, simulated packet loss, room IR, mic noise) applied and configurable.
- [ ] Synthetic audio + ground-truth script artifacts stored under `mvp2/` (or reproducible regeneration command).
- [ ] Metric runner emits DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against synthetic frame-perfect labels.

**MVP3 — honest characterization + pack close:**
- [ ] Anchor set (AMI headset mix or VoxConverse subset, ~30 min) wired into the evaluator.
- [ ] Anchor numbers are within published-baseline ballpark for the chosen diarizer (sanity check on the harness).
- [ ] Overlap-injection mutator exercised on at least one MVP2 corpus item; results recorded.
- [ ] At least one alternative diarizer (Diart or NeMo online clustering / Sortformer) wired into the swap composition root and characterized.
- [ ] Per-diarizer-backend DER/cpWER/latency table recorded.
- [ ] Synthetic-vs-anchor delta reported with a written interpretation.
- [ ] Bot-audio-pipeline extractability audit recorded (clean seam confirmed, or required decoupling work itemized).
- [ ] Retroactive-correction UX decision documented (live-with-updates vs buffered-commit).
- [ ] 3 YouTube eyeball passes recorded with qualitative notes.
- [ ] Hardenloop run for the pack (RnD-only blast radius noted; no production exposure asserted).
sections_by_key.stitching-risk-notes:
- Low stitch risk: the pack's only product-code change is one new seam inside the bot, with metadata that downstream consumers ignore in stage 1. The new seam is additive; existing single-channel transcription path continues unchanged across every MVP.
- Walking-skeleton property is load-bearing: every MVP must remain demoable after the next is layered in. CI / smoke for MVP0 must not regress when MVP1's file adapter lands; MVP0 + MVP1 must not regress when MVP2's synthetic generator lands; etc. PR-time check should re-run each MVP's demo or smoke.
- The RnD harness, oracle, synthetic generator, evaluator, and dashboard are net-new files under their own subtree and do not collide with other packs by surface.
- Risk to watch: if the bot-audio-extractability audit (MVP3 deliverable) finds entanglement and stage 1 has to refactor the audio path to expose a clean seam, the diff stops being additive and starts touching shared bot lifecycle code. In that case, the refactor must be small, reviewed independently, called out in the PR, and probably committed against MVP0's deliverables (since the seam is MVP0's contract) — do not co-mingle it with later-MVP wiring under a single commit.
- Risk to watch: diarizer dependency footprint (pyannote, torch, possibly NeMo) can balloon the bot image in later stages. Stage 1 runs hot-reload outside the container so this does not bite here, but pack notes (MVP3 deliverable) must record the dependency set chosen so stage 2 can plan packaging.
- This epic is not a release sign-off; it is the delivery contract for one independently reviewable research pack whose output feeds stage 2 (identity binding) design.
sections_by_key.synthetic-validation-gate:
PRIMARY gate for the pack, laddered across MVPs:
- **MVP0:** not exercised — MVP0 is human-eyeball only.
- **MVP1:** pseudo-oracle metrics — DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against offline pyannote + Whisper-large on a small real-audio set (3+ inputs: panel, podcast, debate). Recorded under `mvp1/`.
- **MVP2:** frame-perfect synthetic metrics — DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against the oracle-seeded TTS-regenerated corpus with augmentation. Recorded under `mvp2/`.
- **MVP3:** anchor + mutator + multi-diarizer — same metrics against AMI/VoxConverse subset (sanity), against the overlap-injection-mutated subset (overlap stress), and per-diarizer-backend (at minimum: pyannote 3.x streaming + one alternative). Synthetic-vs-anchor delta reported. Recorded under `mvp3/`.
- **Pack-close pass criterion:** all three (MVP1/MVP2/MVP3) metric tiers produced and recorded; no fixed numeric threshold in stage 1, but the delta-to-anchor must be reported with a stated interpretation.
sections_by_key.user-outcome: From MVP0 onward, a user can share any YouTube tab in the RnD dashboard and see a real-time diarized transcript with stable `speaker_0..speaker_N` labels. Each later MVP keeps that demo working while adding rigor underneath it.

source.body:
# Pack Epic: MS Teams local in-bot diarization — research stage 1 (single-channel speaker labels, hot-reload RnD harness, MVP-laddered delivery)

## CEO outcome

Vexa has a credible internal answer to MS Teams' weakest user-visible quality complaint (mis-attributed or unattributed speakers in transcripts), delivered in MVP-laddered increments so a working demo exists from MVP0 onward and every later MVP additively raises rigor — reproducible numbers (MVP1), frame-perfect ground truth (MVP2), and defensible diarizer-backend characterization (MVP3) — without ever leaving 'walking skeleton' state.

## CTO outcome

Each MVP is a full minimal product on its own (demo → autonomous metric → real ground truth → characterized backend choice). The production bot's audio pipeline carries a local diarization seam from MVP0; the RnD harness around it runs production code hot-reload outside the container; stage 2 (identity binding / TS-VAD / per-speaker routing) inherits a quantified floor, an extractability audit, a documented retroactive-correction UX decision, and a defensible backend pick — without architectural drift.

## User outcome

From MVP0 onward, a user can share any YouTube tab in the RnD dashboard and see a real-time diarized transcript with stable `speaker_0..speaker_N` labels. Each later MVP keeps that demo working while adding rigor underneath it.

## Included raw issues / PRs

- No raw GitHub issues; this pack is a forward-looking research epic synthesized from a design conversation on MS Teams single-channel transcription + speaker attribution limits.
- Adjacent context (informational, not in scope): existing MS Teams bot audio buffering + transcription-service call path; existing gmeet/zoom per-participant channel routing path (kept as the target architecture once stage 2 introduces per-speaker routing).

## Explicitly out of scope

- Identity binding to real participant names (Teams bluebox / roster / Copilot signals). Stage 2.
- Target-Speaker VAD (TS-VAD) and per-speaker channel routing in the bot. Stage 2.
- Changes to the transcription service. The service is called unchanged via the bot's existing client; Whisper weights stay inside the service.
- Production Teams bot integration / rollout / feature flagging.
- Container, Compose, Helm, Lite packaging of the RnD harness. The harness is hot-reload of production bot code outside any container; packaging is stage 2+.
- Setting a latency target. Latency is reported as an output (p50/p99 per backend), not gated as a pass/fail in this pack.
- gmeet / zoom diarization changes. Single-channel-only problem here.
- Live bot capture wiring of the third adapter. The adapter seam is defined; only tab-capture (MVP0) and file-replay (MVP1+) adapters are exercised in this pack.

## Blast radius

- MS Teams bot audio pipeline: one new seam (diarization step) between the existing single-channel buffer and the transcription-service client. Output of the seam is `speaker_0..N` labels attached as metadata; the bytes sent to the transcription service are unchanged in stage 1.
- New RnD harness around the bot's audio slice (hot-reload runner + adapters + dashboard + autonomous evaluator). Lives in the repo but does NOT ship in any container.
- New evaluation tooling (MVP1+): pseudo-oracle metric runner (MVP1), oracle-seeded synthetic corpus generator (MVP2: offline pyannote + Whisper-large + voice-cloned TTS + augmentation), anchor + mutator + multi-diarizer (MVP3).
- No change to: transcription service, gmeet/zoom paths, dashboard product UI (RnD dashboard is a separate surface), Compose/Lite/Helm deploy artifacts, public APIs, DB schemas.
- Support / on-call surface: none (RnD only, no production users touch this in stage 1).

## Data / schema / API / public-contract decisions

- Public APIs: no change. Transcription service interface, bot ↔ service RPC, dashboard product API all unchanged in stage 1.
- Internal contract (NEW, lands at MVP0): a stable seam inside the bot exposing `audio_frames_in (pcm, sample_rate, wall_clock_ts) → (segments, speaker_labels speaker_0..N, transcripts)`. Same codepath behind adapters added across MVPs (MVP0: tab capture; MVP1: file replay; live-bot stub at the seam from MVP0 but not wired). Diarizer backend swappable at one composition root from MVP0; alternatives wired in MVP3.
- Metadata: transcription requests/results carry an optional `speaker_label` field populated by the diarization step. Stage 1 keeps this internal to the bot/RnD harness — not exposed downstream.
- No DB schema changes. No new public endpoints. No breaking changes to any service contract.
- Decision still open inside the pack (must be answered by MVP3 close): retroactive-correction UX — does the dashboard render live (sometimes-wrong) labels and update them as the diarizer refines, or buffer and only emit committed labels? MVP0 ships whichever default is fastest to build; MVP3 records the deliberate choice.

## Isolation requirements

- One branch / worktree for this pack only.
- Evidence root: `.agents/packs/pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd/`.
- Per-MVP evidence subfolders under the evidence root (`mvp0/`, `mvp1/`, `mvp2/`, `mvp3/`) so each MVP's deliverables are independently auditable and the pack PR can show the additive layering.
- Runtime namespace: `pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd` (used only for any local processes / ports the harness spins up; no Compose/Lite/Helm namespacing required).
- Hot-reload harness runs production bot code paths byte-identically — no parallel prototype implementation of the audio pipeline, at any MVP.
- Diarization model weights cached across reloads (process-supervisor pattern or equivalent) so iteration is not bottlenecked by model load. Required from MVP0.
- Transcription service is a stable backend dependency: harness points the bot's existing transcription-service client at a local or shared dev instance. Service is not modified, redeployed, or forked by this pack.
- Do not merge another pack into this lane to make any MVP pass.
- Do not introduce identity-binding / TS-VAD code to make a metric move; those are stage 2 and entering them here invalidates the floor characterization this pack is supposed to deliver.

## MVP ladder

Delivery is a strict additive ladder. Each MVP is a fully demoable minimal product on its own — the pack can stop at any MVP if signal is sufficient — and every later MVP runs on top of the previous one's working artifacts. No parallel branches, no "will be integrated later" debt.

### MVP0 — Walking skeleton (end-to-end demo, no metrics)

- **Includes:** hot-reload harness over production bot code; diarization seam inside the bot; one diarizer wired (pyannote 3.x streaming) with the swap composition root in place; tab-capture adapter (browser → harness); live-bot adapter as a stub at the same seam (not wired); RnD dashboard rendering live `speaker_0..N` labels + transcripts via the bot's existing transcription-service client.
- **Excludes:** file-replay adapter, metrics, synthetic corpus, anchor set, alternative diarizers, extractability audit deliverable, retroactive-correction UX decision.
- **Demo:** user shares 1 YouTube panel video; dashboard shows live diarized transcript with plausible speaker boundaries.
- **Stoppable if:** the demo is an obvious go/no-go for the whole research direction. No metrics needed to call it.
- **Evidence:** `mvp0/` — harness command, dashboard screenshot or short capture, bot-seam diff summary.

### MVP1 — Autonomous iteration (add metrics on real audio)

- **Includes:** file-replay adapter (byte-identical codepath to tab-capture, same composition root); pseudo-oracle metric runner — DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 measured against the offline pyannote + Whisper-large output on a small real-audio set (3+ inputs: panel, podcast, debate).
- **Excludes:** synthetic regeneration, anchor set, alternative diarizers, mutators.
- **Demo:** every change produces a number. Codex can iterate without a human watching.
- **Stoppable if:** pseudo-oracle numbers alone are convincing enough to make the stage 2 call. Synthetic frame-perfect ground truth is nice-to-have, not required, if the qualitative MVP0 demo plus MVP1 numbers already justify stage 2.
- **Evidence:** `mvp1/` — metric runner command, results table, input audio manifest, pseudo-oracle output artifacts.

### MVP2 — Real ground truth (oracle-as-script-generator)

- **Includes:** script extraction from MVP1's pseudo-oracle output → voice-cloned TTS per speaker (XTTS-v2 / F5-TTS / equivalent) → original-timing stitch → augmentation pass (Opus codec round-trip at Teams bitrate, simulated packet loss, room IR convolution, mic noise floor). Synthetic audio's ground truth IS the script by construction. Metric runner now evaluates against true labels on synthetic.
- **Excludes:** anchor set, mutators, alternative diarizers.
- **Demo:** synthetic corpus exists, reproducibly regeneratable, evaluator emits DER/cpWER/latency against frame-perfect labels.
- **Stoppable if:** synthetic numbers are strong enough to architect stage 2 confidently. Multi-diarizer comparison can be deferred to stage 2's planning if MVP2's chosen default is already in the right ballpark.
- **Evidence:** `mvp2/` — generator command, synthetic corpus WAV + ground-truth scripts (or reproducible regeneration command), evaluator results on synthetic.

### MVP3 — Honest characterization + pack close

- **Includes:** anchor set (AMI headset mix or VoxConverse subset, ~30 min total) wired into the evaluator; overlap-injection mutator exercised on at least one MVP2 corpus item; at least one alternative diarizer (Diart or NeMo online clustering / Sortformer) wired into the swap composition root and characterized; bot-audio-pipeline extractability audit recorded; retroactive-correction UX decision picked and documented; 3 YouTube eyeball passes recorded with qualitative notes; Hardenloop run for the pack; PR opened against the pack's integration branch.
- **Excludes:** stage 2 work (identity binding, TS-VAD, per-speaker routing, container packaging).
- **Demo:** per-diarizer DER/cpWER/latency table on synthetic + anchor + mutated-overlap subset; synthetic-vs-anchor delta reported; extractability audit and UX decision are written-down artifacts in the PR.
- **Stoppable if:** this is the pack-close MVP. Stopping here = pack delivered.
- **Evidence:** `mvp3/` — backend comparison table, anchor results, mutator results, extractability audit note, UX decision note, Hardenloop output, eyeball-pass notes.

## Compose validation gate

Not required at any MVP. This pack does not modify any Compose-shipped service contract; the RnD harness runs outside Compose. If MVP3's final diff incidentally touches a file the Compose stack also imports, run the standard Compose smoke as a regression check; otherwise mark explicitly not required in PR evidence.

## Lite validation gate

Not required at any MVP. No Lite-relevant surface changes. Mark explicitly not required in PR evidence.

## Synthetic validation gate

PRIMARY gate for the pack, laddered across MVPs:
- **MVP0:** not exercised — MVP0 is human-eyeball only.
- **MVP1:** pseudo-oracle metrics — DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against offline pyannote + Whisper-large on a small real-audio set (3+ inputs: panel, podcast, debate). Recorded under `mvp1/`.
- **MVP2:** frame-perfect synthetic metrics — DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against the oracle-seeded TTS-regenerated corpus with augmentation. Recorded under `mvp2/`.
- **MVP3:** anchor + mutator + multi-diarizer — same metrics against AMI/VoxConverse subset (sanity), against the overlap-injection-mutated subset (overlap stress), and per-diarizer-backend (at minimum: pyannote 3.x streaming + one alternative). Synthetic-vs-anchor delta reported. Recorded under `mvp3/`.
- **Pack-close pass criterion:** all three (MVP1/MVP2/MVP3) metric tiers produced and recorded; no fixed numeric threshold in stage 1, but the delta-to-anchor must be reported with a stated interpretation.

## Live / human validation gate

Required, laddered across MVPs:
- **MVP0:** 1 YouTube video eyeball pass via tab-capture → dashboard. Qualitative: is the demo plausible enough to keep building on?
- **MVP1, MVP2:** no additional human gate (autonomous-loop MVPs).
- **MVP3:** 3 distinct YouTube videos (panel, podcast, debate-style) via tab-capture; user judges, per video, whether speaker boundaries and transcript text are coherent enough to demonstrate the approach. Pass/fail recorded with short notes.
- No production Teams meeting test in this pack at any MVP. Live-Teams validation belongs to stage 3 (production integration).

## PR readiness checklist

Checklist is MVP-gated. The pack PR aggregates evidence across all MVPs.

**Cross-cutting (must hold at MVP3 / PR time):**
- [ ] Pack branch starts from the agreed base and stays scoped to this pack only.
- [ ] Bot audio pipeline modified at exactly one seam; no parallel prototype path; production bot code is what the harness runs at every MVP.
- [ ] Out-of-scope items confirmed absent from the diff (no bluebox, no TS-VAD, no per-speaker routing, no transcription-service changes).
- [ ] Per-MVP evidence subfolders (`mvp0/`, `mvp1/`, `mvp2/`, `mvp3/`) exist under `.agents/packs/pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd/` and contain the deliverables listed below.
- [ ] PR body links this epic and the evidence root.

**MVP0 — walking skeleton:**
- [ ] Hot-reload harness over production bot code is runnable; model weights cache across reloads.
- [ ] Diarization seam inside the bot exists at one composition root; swap point for backends is in place.
- [ ] pyannote 3.x streaming wired as the default diarizer.
- [ ] Tab-capture adapter wired; live-bot adapter present as a stub at the same seam (not wired).
- [ ] RnD dashboard renders live `speaker_0..N` + transcript from tab-capture.
- [ ] 1 YouTube eyeball pass recorded.

**MVP1 — autonomous metrics on real audio:**
- [ ] File-replay adapter wired; codepath byte-identical to tab-capture.
- [ ] Pseudo-oracle (offline pyannote + Whisper-large) producible reproducibly on the input set.
- [ ] Metric runner emits DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against pseudo-oracle on 3+ real-audio inputs.
- [ ] Results table recorded under `mvp1/`.

**MVP2 — frame-perfect ground truth:**
- [ ] Script extraction from pseudo-oracle output runs end-to-end.
- [ ] Voice-cloned TTS per speaker + original-timing stitcher produces synthetic WAV.
- [ ] Augmentation pass (Opus codec round-trip, simulated packet loss, room IR, mic noise) applied and configurable.
- [ ] Synthetic audio + ground-truth script artifacts stored under `mvp2/` (or reproducible regeneration command).
- [ ] Metric runner emits DER, cpWER, latency p50/p99 against synthetic frame-perfect labels.

**MVP3 — honest characterization + pack close:**
- [ ] Anchor set (AMI headset mix or VoxConverse subset, ~30 min) wired into the evaluator.
- [ ] Anchor numbers are within published-baseline ballpark for the chosen diarizer (sanity check on the harness).
- [ ] Overlap-injection mutator exercised on at least one MVP2 corpus item; results recorded.
- [ ] At least one alternative diarizer (Diart or NeMo online clustering / Sortformer) wired into the swap composition root and characterized.
- [ ] Per-diarizer-backend DER/cpWER/latency table recorded.
- [ ] Synthetic-vs-anchor delta reported with a written interpretation.
- [ ] Bot-audio-pipeline extractability audit recorded (clean seam confirmed, or required decoupling work itemized).
- [ ] Retroactive-correction UX decision documented (live-with-updates vs buffered-commit).
- [ ] 3 YouTube eyeball passes recorded with qualitative notes.
- [ ] Hardenloop run for the pack (RnD-only blast radius noted; no production exposure asserted).

## Stitching risk notes

- Low stitch risk: the pack's only product-code change is one new seam inside the bot, with metadata that downstream consumers ignore in stage 1. The new seam is additive; existing single-channel transcription path continues unchanged across every MVP.
- Walking-skeleton property is load-bearing: every MVP must remain demoable after the next is layered in. CI / smoke for MVP0 must not regress when MVP1's file adapter lands; MVP0 + MVP1 must not regress when MVP2's synthetic generator lands; etc. PR-time check should re-run each MVP's demo or smoke.
- The RnD harness, oracle, synthetic generator, evaluator, and dashboard are net-new files under their own subtree and do not collide with other packs by surface.
- Risk to watch: if the bot-audio-extractability audit (MVP3 deliverable) finds entanglement and stage 1 has to refactor the audio path to expose a clean seam, the diff stops being additive and starts touching shared bot lifecycle code. In that case, the refactor must be small, reviewed independently, called out in the PR, and probably committed against MVP0's deliverables (since the seam is MVP0's contract) — do not co-mingle it with later-MVP wiring under a single commit.
- Risk to watch: diarizer dependency footprint (pyannote, torch, possibly NeMo) can balloon the bot image in later stages. Stage 1 runs hot-reload outside the container so this does not bite here, but pack notes (MVP3 deliverable) must record the dependency set chosen so stage 2 can plan packaging.
- This epic is not a release sign-off; it is the delivery contract for one independently reviewable research pack whose output feeds stage 2 (identity binding) design.

## Pack metadata

- Pack id: `pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd`
- Release: `TBD — research pack, not yet bound to a numbered Vexa release`
- Base branch: `main`
- Integration branch: `main`
- Runtime namespace: `pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd`
- Evidence root: `.agents/packs/pack-msteams-local-diarization-rnd/`
source.title: [Pack] MS Teams local in-bot diarization — research stage 1 (single-channel speaker labels, hot-reload RnD harness, MVP-laddered delivery)

title: [Pack] MS Teams local in-bot diarization — research stage 1 (single-channel speaker labels, hot-reload RnD harness, MVP-laddered delivery)
